item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the main article much more than the supplementals. There was just enough info in the logs to make you speculate without giving too much away. The supplemental pages on the other hand simultaneously left me confused and felt like infodumps. Up vote for the main, no vote for the other two.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The execution supercedes the plot in this case, I guess. On the surface, it's just another creepy school with changing contents, like multiple things in Series 1. The Anabasis device and its relation to the school don't make much sense until reading the extra files (and the relevant Manna Charitable material, I guess, although if Rhiannon is working for the MCF, shouldn't the Foundation have discovered that when looking into her?).

A hesitant upvote - it's a compelling story, but I don't like having to breadcrumb into Tales or GOI files to find the ending =P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The email coorrespondence makes it feel a lot more like what you're describing. After reading the emails, I fully understood everything that you had written above, before I had ever read the stuff you wrote above.

The issue is, before I read the email coorrespondence, I just assumed that it was all some crazy hallucinatory effect, and that Agent Locke was a part of that hallucinatory effect. The coorrespondence was absolutely necessary to clear it up.

Stil, I like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the article. I like the email correspondence. I think the emails detract from the article. It's spookier without that explanation.

Upvoted both, but I think the email correspondences should be left as a separate tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Got something close to that - I thought the Anabasis was a reality rewriting thing that managed to change history to our own by letting that school behind.
No matter what, I like it. Begins as a haunted house story that develops into something more original.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought it all was pretty great. I didn't at first realize what the Anabasis was (but I think that was more of a me problem than a problem with the writing), but after reading in your spoilers bit what it was, it all makes sense and I really enjoyed this story. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Either I'm being too subtle or I'm stupid (most likely one of those)..

SPOILERS
It will never be your birthday again

The real agents are catching glimpses of what was happening in the school—kids being vaccinated against this bio-weapon, the dead being cremated, etc.

The fictional agent is (or was) on-location to the actual events, describing the effects of the bio-weapon, and the botched vaccinations. Due to time-space shenanigans, the real agents caught brief glimpses, and one of the agents managed to slip his report into our reality.

The implication was that this school building was being used to test a vaccine against the bio-weapon, but then became an emergency aid station due to war with an unknown enemy. The point of the "Anabasis" was to shift the school building (and presumably other buildings) into an alternate reality in order to avoid the war and the spread of the bio-weapon (as presumably they had contained it within the school) thus giving them either more time to work out a vaccine, or at least let the uninfected escape alive.

Except the people keep blinking in and out of existence, with inconsistent chronologies, indicating the "Anabasis" either failed or wasn't thoroughly tested before use.

If none of that got through, I'm sorry. Maybe should I dump more exposition into the last log, or re-work the explorations to be more informative?

EDIT: I did just that:

http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-2084-email-correspondence

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really know how it connects at all with the rest of the article, but the last log was intriguing enough to be worth an upvote on its own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote for now because I simply don't get it. I'll give it a shot tomorrow when I've had some sleep but this is one of the most confusing skips I've read in a while and could possibly do with a read-over by the author from a different perspective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm intrigued, since it seems to have a different effect for each person, and I like the layered perspectives. However, I'm still confused regarding what you're trying to accomplish—is the anomaly the "substance", the building, the perceptive effects, the phantom agent? Is this a Poltergeist/indian burial ground thing where some kind of outbreak was covered up at the site and now people are experiencing echoes of it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do like that, for once, the agents at no point seem to be in danger in there. It just lets the creepiness and WTF stand on its own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice creepy exploration logs, though I'm honestly not too sure how the last one fits in.

One thing I'd like to see clarified is the description. Saying the school "contains a substance" is kind of non-specific. From the logs, it seems to be something permeating the atmosphere, but before that point, I'm going, okay, is the school filled with something, is it coating all surfaces, does it just sit in a beaker in one room, what?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You forgot the "#", and you ain't letting me add it.

E: Added it per te anyway.
